reviewComment: Would pinning this image to a digest make production builds reproducible while still allowing automated dependency updates?
rejectedCode: FROM node:22-alpine AS build
acceptedCode:
FROM node:22-alpine AS build
RUN npm run build
FROM node:22-alpine AS runtime
ENV NODE_ENV=production
COPY --from=build /app/package*.json ./
COPY --from=build /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=build /app/dist ./dist
CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]

semanticAnalysis.rule: Would pinning this image to a digest make production builds reproducible while still allowing automated dependency updates?
semanticAnalysis.rationale: The accepted implementation replaced the reviewed pattern with the preferred pattern.

acceptedCode:
# SignalDesk
SignalDesk is a TypeScript service for coordinating operational incidents.
It provides a small HTTP API for incidents, responder notes, and service health.
The service listens on `PORT` (default `3000`) and stores data in memory.
See `docs/api.md` for endpoints and `docs/operations.md` for deployment guidance.

# SignalDesk
SignalDesk is a TypeScript service for coordinating operational incidents.
It provides a small HTTP API for incidents, responder notes, and service health.
## Development
```bash
npm install
npm run dev
npm test
The service listens on `PORT` (default `3000`) and stores data in memory.
See `docs/api.md` for endpoints and `docs/operations.md` for deployment guidance.

# SignalDesk product brief

SignalDesk is a small incident coordination API for engineering teams.
acceptedCode:
# SignalDesk product brief
SignalDesk is a small incident coordination API for engineering teams.
- Expose health and service metrics for operators.
The first release intentionally uses an in-memory store so the team can validate the API before choosing persistent storage.

semanticAnalysis.title: Could we call out whether this API is intended for internal responders only
semanticAnalysis.rule: Could we call out whether this API is intended for internal responders only? That boundary will influence authentication decisions later.
semanticAnalysis.rationale: The accepted implementation replaced the reviewed pattern with the preferred pattern.

reviewComment: Should assignment be part of the first milestone, or is naming an incident commander enough for the initial workflow?
rejectedCode:
# SignalDesk product brief

SignalDesk is a small incident coordination API for engineering teams.
It keeps active incidents, status changes, and responder notes in one place.

## Initial goals

- Create and assign operational incidents.
acceptedCode:
# SignalDesk product brief
SignalDesk is a small incident coordination API for engineering teams.
It keeps active incidents, status changes, and responder notes in one place.
## Initial goals
- Create and assign operational incidents.
- Track severity and lifecycle transitions.
- Record timestamped notes during mitigation.
- Expose health and service metrics for operators.
The first release intentionally uses an in-memory store so the team can validate the API before choosing persistent storage.

semanticAnalysis.title: Should assignment be part of the first milestone, or is naming an incident comma
semanticAnalysis.rule: Should assignment be part of the first milestone, or is naming an incident commander enough for the initial workflow?
semanticAnalysis.rationale: The accepted implementation replaced the reviewed pattern with the preferred pattern.

reviewComment: It may be worth stating explicitly that restarts discard active incidents so demo users are not surprised by the tradeoff.

acceptedCode:
# SignalDesk product brief
SignalDesk is a small incident coordination API for engineering teams.
It keeps active incidents, status changes, and responder notes in one place.
## Initial goals
- Create and assign operational incidents.
- Track severity and lifecycle transitions.
- Record timestamped notes during mitigation.
- Expose health and service metrics for operators.
The first release intentionally uses an in-memory store so the team can validate the API before choosing persistent storage.
